# Tasting Journal of 2 Wines and 1 Paired with Food CB

Tasting Journal of 2 Wines and 1 Paired with Food

HSPT.161.02 | Chase BalmerTop of Form

# Instructions Bottom of Form

Add at least 3 descriptors per "S" box for the two wines you try. These two wines should not be wines we have already tried in class or listed on the following weeks' wine lists. When you pair the food with one of the wines, add notes on what the food is, how the wine changes in the mouth and finish, and if it was a good pairing in your opinion. Then submit this to the myCourses Assignment section.



# Wine 1: Ancient Peaks Cabernet Sauvignon

Paso Robles, California

Santa Margarita Ranch

2021

14.3%

$20



## Food Pairing

Prosciutto:

This pairing was alright. Eating the prosciutto prior to sipping the wine reduced the intensity of the bite of the wine - the acid was less intense. Although the bite was mellowed, this pairing did not significantly elevate either component. I much preferred the following pairing ->



Parmesan:

This pairing was fantastic. Coating my mouth with the cheese and spreading the flavor of the wine extended the hearty/nutty flavor of the cheese. I felt like I was able to enjoy the cheese in a way I wouldn’t regularly. Regarding the wine, the pairing brought forward the dark fruit flavors. Like the prior, this pairing made the acidity / bite less aggressive.

# Wine 2: Bully Hill Vineyards Chardonnay Riesling Fusion

Finger Lakes White Wine

Chardonnay 60%, Riseling 40%

NV

11.5%
